title: Ensure That Cloud SQL Database Instances Are Configured  With Automated Backups

input_row.title: Ensure That Cloud SQL Database Instances Are Configured  With Automated Backups
input_row.description: It is recommended to have all SQL database instances set to enable automated backups.
input_row.rationale: Backups provide a way to restore a Cloud SQL instance to recover lost data or recover from a problem with that instance. Automated backups need to be set for any instance that contains data that should be protected from loss or damage. This recommendation is applicable for SQL Server, PostgreSql, MySql generation 1 and MySql generation 2 instances.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the Cloud SQL Instances page in the Google Cloud Console by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/sql/instances. 2. Click the instance name to open its instance details page. 3. Go to the Backups menu. 4. Ensure that Automated backups is set to Enabled and Backup time is mentioned. From Google Cloud CLI 1. List all Cloud SQL database instances using the following command: gcloud sql instances list --format=json | jq '. | map(select(.instanceType != "READ_REPLICA_INSTANCE")) | .[].name' NOTE: gcloud command has been added with the filter to exclude read-replicas instances, as GCP do not provide Automated Backups for read-replica instances. 2. Ensure that the below command returns True for every Cloud SQL database instance. gcloud sql instances describe -- format="value('Enabled':settings.backupConfiguration.enabled)"
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the Cloud SQL Instances page in the Google Cloud Console by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/sql/instances. 2. Select the instance where the backups need to be configured. 3. Click Edit. 4. In the Backups section, check `Enable automated backups', and choose a backup window. 5. Click Save. From Google Cloud CLI 1. List all Cloud SQL database instances using the following command: gcloud sql instances list --format=json | jq '. | map(select(.instanceType != "READ_REPLICA_INSTANCE")) | .[].name' NOTE: gcloud command has been added with the filter to exclude read-replicas instances, as GCP do not provide Automated Backups for read-replica instances. 2. Enable Automated backups for every Cloud SQL database instance using the below command: gcloud sql instances patch --backup-start-time <[HH:MM]> The backup-start-time parameter is specified in 24-hour time, in the UTC±00 time zone, and specifies the start of a 4-hour backup window. Backups can start any time during the backup window.
input_row.impact: Automated Backups will increase required size of storage and costs associated with it.
input_row.default_value: By default, automated backups are not configured for Cloud SQL instances.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit details provide both Google Cloud Console instructions and the CLI commands necessary to determine if automated backups are enabled for Cloud SQL instances.
- The use of the `gcloud sql instances list` and `gcloud sql instances describe` commands makes it possible to verify the settings programmatically.
- The control strictly relates to a standard GCP service configuration, which can be queried via the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) and the GCP APIs.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_sql_instance_automated_backups_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
- Use the following Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) command to list all Cloud SQL instances, excluding read-replica instances:
   ```
   gcloud sql instances list --format=json | jq '. | map(select(.instanceType != "READ_REPLICA_INSTANCE")) | .[].name'
   ```

- For each Cloud SQL instance returned from the previous command, use the following command to check if automated backups are enabled:
   ```
   gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME> --format="value(settings.backupConfiguration.enabled)"
   ```
   Replace `<INSTANCE_NAME>` with the name of each instance retrieved.

- **Pass Criteria:** If the `gcloud` command for each instance returns `True`, automated backups are enabled.
- **Fail Criteria:** If any instance returns `False`, that instance does not have automated backups enabled.

- Ensure to review the `settings.backupConfiguration.enabled` field in the output, which indicates if automated backups are correctly configured. Each instance should be checked in accordance with the exclusion of read-replica instances as noted in the audit description.
